title: ActiveMQ KahaDB Journal Disk Pressure Before Broker Stall
dataSources: `index=messaging` `sourcetype=activemq:log` (`activemq.log` on the host that owns the KahaDB directory), `sourcetype=activemq:broker` JMX `org.apache.activemq:type=Broker` `StorePercentUsage` / `StoreLimit` when using KahaDB persistence
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder (file monitor); Splunk Add-on for JMX (Splunkbase 2647) or Jolokiaâ†’HEC for `activemq:broker`
spl:
index=messaging (sourcetype="activemq:log" OR sourcetype="activemq:broker") earliest=-24h
| eval store_pct=coalesce(StorePercentUsage, round(store_used*100/nullif(store_limit,0),1))
| eval journal_signal=if(sourcetype="activemq:log" AND match(_raw, "(?i)(No space left on device|IOException.*(journal|kahadb)|Persistence store.*(full|limit)|KahaDB.*(ERROR|failed))"), 1, 0)
| eval is_jdbc=if(like(lower(coalesce(persistence_adapter, PersistenceAdapterName, "")), "%jdbc%"), 1, 0)
| where journal_signal==1 OR (sourcetype="activemq:broker" AND is_jdbc==0 AND store_pct>=85)
| table _time, host, sourcetype, broker_name, store_pct, journal_signal, is_jdbc, _raw
description: KahaDB writes sequential journal files on disk; when the volume fills or the broker hits `StoreLimit`, producers block and messages stop draining. Combining filesystem-level IO errors from `activemq.log` with `StorePercentUsage` for KahaDB-configured brokers catches the failure mode before complete flow shutdown.
value: Prevents catastrophic messaging outages during storage sprawl or mis-sized data volumes by alerting while the broker can still shed load or expand disk.
implementation: Prefer tagging `persistence_adapter` or `PersistenceAdapterName` at ingest; when null, store-pressure alerts still fireâ€”exclude confirmed JDBC-only brokers via a lookup. Monitor the partition that hosts `activemq_data` at the OS layer in parallel. Set `store_pct` threshold just below your operational runbook limit (often 80â€“90%).
visualization: Single value (max store %), timeline of log matches, table (broker, store_pct, sample log line).

references.title: Apache ActiveMQ â€” JMX reference (Broker MBeans)

grandmaExplanation: We watch the disk space and health of the database files ActiveMQ uses so it does not suddenly freeze when the drive is full or the journal cannot write.
